disorder (ADHD) frequently occuring, brain-based, neurodevelopmental disorder with substantial negative consequences individual public health.
Once viewed childhood condition, recognized that majority cases persist throughout adolescence adulthood.
lifelong impact ADHD often extends beyond disorder's defining features developmentally inappropriate levels inattention and/or hyperactivity impulsivity.
Misinformation about ADHD abounds.
Some assert that ADHD real.
Critics variably maintain that childhood inattention hyperactivity normal, diagnosis subjective, behaviors attributed ADHD arise when parents teachers fail maintain appropriate discipline, older students fake symptoms obtain academic advantages drugs, adults similarly drug-seeking looking excuse various life failures, that condition represents conspiracy pharmaceutical companies organized psychiatry increase medication sales.
This could further from truth.
Indeed, ADHD among most scientifically validated psychiatric disorders.
diagnostic reliability well demonstrated with many conditions general medicine.
Medications ADHD have been used over years millions individuals annually, creating indisputable record real-world safety positive result.
Having ADHD associated with significant added risk developing other mental health disorders, well functional impairments, across range life domains.
These domains include educational attainment, social skills, occupational success, personal relationships, parenting, personal safety, general health.
ADHD associated with increased lifetime risk anxiety, depression, substance abuse, impulse control disorders.
Children with ADHD have increased rates learning disabilities, oppositional defiant disorder, conduct disorder, depression, anxiety, other conditions, compared with unaffected children.
ADHD occurs racial, ethnic, socioeconomic groups function intellectual ability.
Like many psychiatric conditions, causes ADHD multifactorial, involving interaction multiple genes environmental factors.
biological basis ADHD strongly evidenced genetic brain imaging studies.
There growing appreciation extent which interactions between genetic environmental factors increase disease risk.
Although both genetic environmental ADHD risk factors (maternal smoking, increased maternal age, style, parenting conflict, family stress,...) identified, none causal many individuals with them develop disorder.
Evidence suggests that underlying ADHD genetic risks have greater expression context high environmental adversity attenuated with increased psychosocial stability.
Neuropsychological models emphasize deficits cognitive function that consistent with brain imaging abnormalities.
popular neuropsychological model suggests that ADHD arises from primary deficits executive functions (EFs), generally defined cognitive processes that support appropriate problem-solving skills aimed towards achieving future goals.
Specifically, maintain information about possible choices working memory facilitate integration with other relevant information from current environmental context order make optimal choices given situation.
deficits ADHD include working memory, response inhibition, general weaknesses executive control.
Executive function (EF) processes largely function prefrontal lobes, which implicated further with behavioral hyperactivity, impulsivity, inattention.
Impaired appears play important role complex neuropsychology ADHD, does adequately explain cases condition.
what diagnostic criteria adhd?
Despite demonstrated biological difference groups with without ADHD, condition remains behaviorally defined syndrome diagnosed careful assessment symptoms clinical history, laboratory tests brain imaging.
ADHD diagnosed basis persistent difficulties from symptoms inattention and/or that occur greater degree than expected individual's developmental level.
DSM-5 retains same ADHD symptoms used DSM-IV, nine inattentive nine
According criteria DSM-5, persistent impaired behavior minimum months that exceeds what expected individual's developmental level characterized least inattentive and/or symptoms required make ADHD diagnosis.
individual years older, only symptoms required.
person also must show some history symptoms before least several symptoms must occur more than setting (e.g., home, school, social relations, work, recreational activities, etc.).
symptoms also must result clinically meaningful impairment social, academic, occupational functioning (for example individual negative performance review).
what treatment adhd?
Treatment ADHD children adolescents usually requires team approach that coordinates various interventions office, family, school settings.
This includes services physicians, psychologists, teachers, other mental behavioral health professionals.
multi-modal approaches ADHD treatment children adolescents have proven successful maximizing improved global functioning.
These domains include Psycho-education, Family-Focused interventions, School-Focused interventions (section plans, standardized testing accommodations, behavioral classroom management), Patient-Focused interventions (pharmacotherapy, social skills training, individual psychotherapy).
adults, preliminary evidence supports potential role psycho-social interventions, including psycho-education, cognitive behavioral (CBT) other therapies, school workplace accommodations, coaching, support groups.
Optimal treatment generally combines medication management with problem-specific interventions selected individual patients.
most useful interventions tend brief, well-structured, skill-oriented, specific ADHD.
Treatment algorithms medication selection adults less established than those younger individuals.
approved both stimulant non-stimulant medications adults.
with children, adult clinical trials consistently show larger treatment effect sizes stimulants versus non-stimulants.
Potential benefits stimulant easily assessed with short-term titration.
Larger effect sizes, safety, ease titration support recommendation stimulants first-line adult treatments, similar pediatric ADHD.
Atomoxetine (Strattera) also indicated adults provide second-line alternative stimulants prove unsatisfactory.
FDA-Approved Medications ADHD include stimulants: Methylphenidates, D-Methylphenidates, D-Amphetamines, Mixed Amphetamine Salts (Adderall) Lisdexamfetamine (Vyvanse); nonstimulants: Atomoxetine Extended-release alpha-2 agonists.
FDA-Approved Methylphenidate (MPH) Amphetamine (AMPH) formulations produced immediate release, sustained release extended release.
